headline: Move Over, Musk—The World's Richest Woman Joined the $100B Club
summary: Alice Walton, daughter of Walmart founder Sam Walton, is the richest woman in the world worth more than $100 billion.

headline: Target Leads With Pokémon and Sports Card Sales Spike in 2025
summary: Target leads 2025 sales surge as Pokémon and sports trading cards boom, projecting $1B+ revenue while Walmart logs sharp online gains.

headline: Tech Giants Shift From China, Trump's Beef Pledge, Putin-Trump Tunnel Pitch: This Week In Politics
summary: U.S. tech giants shift production from China as Trump tackles beef prices, rare earths, and fertility drug costs, while a Russian official pitches a Musk-built 'Putin-Trump' tunnel.

headline: Weekend Round-Up: AI Takes Center Stage As Nvidia, Microsoft, Blackrock, Intel And Apple Make Big Moves
summary: Artificial intelligence dominated the week as Nvidia, Microsoft, BlackRock, Intel, and JPMorgan Chase made major AI-driven moves — from a $40 billion Texas data center and new chip launches to bold forecasts and manufacturing shifts out of China.

headline: D-Wave Quantum, Estee Lauder, and Bunge Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (Oct. 13-Oct. 17): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
summary: Top large-cap gainers last week included D-Wave Quantum, Estée Lauder, Bunge, Bloom Energy, Korea Electric Power, J.B. Hunt, SanDisk, Albertsons, ON Semiconductor, and Ubiquiti — boosted by deals, product openings, strong earnings, and analyst upgrades.

headline: Analyst Ming-Chi Kuo Said Nvidia PR Didn't Make It Clear That After First Blackwell Wafer Was Produced In The U.S., It Would Still Need To Be Shipped To Taiwan For CoWoS Advanced Packaging To Be Considered Complete
China May Have Achieved Solid-State Battery Breakthrough With 600-Mile Range: Here's All You Need To Know
summary: Chinese researchers' share breakthrough in EV battery technology with solid-state batteries offering 600+ mile range and weighing 220 lbs.

headline: Consumer Tech News (Oct 13-17): Elon Musk's Starlink Under US Probe, Salesforce Eyes $60B Revenue, Apple Launches M5 Chip And More
summary: U.S. Congress probes Elon Musk's Starlink over Myanmar links as Apple unveils M5 chip, TSMC beats earnings, and Salesforce targets $60B revenue by FY2030.

headline: Exclusive F1 Rights To Steve Jobs' Movie And More: This Week In Appleverse
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang To Meet Global Leaders, Korean Chip Executives At APEC Summit Amid Trade Tensions
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to meet with world leaders and top South Korean business executives at APEC CEO Summit.

headline: Alibaba Cloud's New System Cuts Nvidia GPU Usage By 82%, Amid Trump's Flip Flop On AI Chip Ban On China
summary: Alibaba introduces Aegaeon, a computing pooling system reducing Nvidia GPU reliance by 82%.

headline: Kamala Harris Says Not Inviting Elon Musk To 2021 White House EV Event Was A 'Big Mistake:' Report
summary: Kamala Harris has expressed regret over the exclusion of Tesla's Elon Musk from a 2021 White House electric vehicle event.

headline: As San Francisco Rents Surge, AI Startups Offer Housing Benefits To Keep Workers Close
summary: AI startups like Cluely and Lindy are offering luxury housing and six-figure salaries to compete for top talent amid soaring Bay Area rents.
